questions.question: The ancient city of Mohenjo-daro is located in present-day:
questions.explanation: Mohenjo-daro is located in Sindh province of present-day Pakistan. It was one of the major centres of the Indus Valley Civilization.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as:
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization is also called Harappan Civilization, named after Harappa — one of its major sites (in present-day Pakistan), excavated in 1921.

questions.question: The 'Wavell Plan' (1945) was presented at which conference?
questions.explanation: The Wavell Plan (1945) was presented at the Shimla Conference. It proposed that the Executive Council of the Viceroy would have equal representation of Hindus and Muslims.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilisation is also called:
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilisation is also called the Harappan Civilisation, named after Harappa — the first site to be excavated (1921). It was one of the four ancient river valley civilisations.

questions.question: Harappa was excavated for the first time by:
questions.explanation: Harappa was first excavated by Daya Ram Sahni in 1921. Mohenjo-daro was excavated by R.D. Banerji in 1922. Sir John Marshall was the Director General of ASI who coordinated the excavations.

questions.question: Mohenjo-daro is located in present-day:
questions.explanation: Mohenjo-daro (meaning 'Mound of the Dead') is located in Sindh province, Pakistan, on the right bank of the Indus River. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: The 'Great Bath' found at Mohenjo-daro was:
options: A large public water tank, possibly used for ritual bathing
questions.explanation: The Great Bath (11.88m × 7m, 2.43m deep) was a large public bath, possibly used for ritual purification. It was made watertight with burnt bricks and bitumen. One of the most famous structures of the IVC.

questions.question: Which Indus Valley site is famous for a dockyard?
questions.explanation: Lothal (Gujarat) had a unique dockyard (214m × 36m) — the earliest known in the world. It connected to the Sabarmati river, suggesting advanced maritime trade. Also famous for bead-making factories.

questions.question: The Indus Valley people worshipped:
options: Mother goddess, Pashupati (proto-Shiva), and animals like bull and unicorn
questions.explanation: IVC people worshipped a Mother Goddess (fertility), Pashupati (a seated figure surrounded by animals — proto-Shiva), trees (peepal), and animals. Figurines and seals show these religious elements.

questions.question: The script of the Indus Valley Civilisation is:
questions.explanation: The Indus script is a pictographic/logo-syllabic script that has not been deciphered. Over 400 signs have been identified. Without a bilingual inscription like the Rosetta Stone, scholars cannot read it.

questions.question: The standard unit of weight used by the Harappans was:
options: Based on the binary system (doubling: 1,2,4,8,16,32,64) and then decimal
questions.explanation: Harappan weights followed a binary system up to 64, then decimal: 1,2,4,8,16,32,64,160,200,320,640. Cuboid chert weights were found across sites, suggesting standardised trade.

questions.question: Kalibangan, an Indus Valley site, is located in:
questions.explanation: Kalibangan is located in Hanumangarh district, Rajasthan, on the Ghaggar (ancient Saraswati) river. Famous for fire altars, ploughed fields (earliest evidence of ploughing), and pre-Harappan settlement.

questions.question: The decline of the Indus Valley Civilisation (c. 1900 BCE) was most likely caused by:
options: A combination of factors: climate change, flooding, river course changes, and possible epidemic
Complete drying up of all rivers
questions.explanation: Most modern scholars believe the IVC decline was multicausal: climate change causing drought, shifts in monsoon patterns, flooding, river course changes (Ghaggar-Hakra drying up), and possible trade collapse. The 'Aryan invasion' theory alone is now rejected.

questions.question: The Indus Valley people traded with:
options: Mesopotamia (present-day Iraq) — seals found in Ur and other Mesopotamian sites
questions.explanation: IVC traded with Mesopotamia (Sumer, Akkad). Harappan seals, weights, and carnelian beads found at Ur. Mesopotamian texts mention 'Meluhha' — believed to refer to the Indus region.

questions.question: Which crop was NOT cultivated by the Indus Valley people?
questions.explanation: Maize is a New World crop that reached India only after Columbus (post-1492). Indus people cultivated wheat, barley, cotton (first in the world!), rice (Lothal), dates, sesame, and mustard.

questions.question: Cotton was first cultivated in the world by:
questions.explanation: Cotton was first cultivated by the Indus Valley people around 3000 BCE. They also produced cotton textiles. Mesopotamians called it 'sindon' (from Sindh). India remained a major cotton producer throughout history.

questions.question: The Rigveda mentions which river most frequently?
questions.explanation: The Saraswati River is the most praised river in the Rigveda, described as the greatest of rivers. The Sindhu (Indus) is also frequently mentioned. The Ganga appears only twice in the Rigveda.

questions.question: The Harappan city known for its large dockyard is:
questions.explanation: Lothal in Gujarat had a well-planned dockyard (170m×37m), indicating maritime trade with Mesopotamia. It is the only Harappan site with a dockyard.

questions.question: Great Bath was found at which Harappan site?
questions.explanation: The Great Bath at Mohenjo-daro (11.88m×7.01m) is the most striking feature of the Indus Valley Civilization, used for ritualistic bathing.

questions.question: The Indus script is:
questions.explanation: The Indus script (Harappan script) remains undeciphered despite many attempts. It contains over 400 signs and was written right-to-left.

questions.question: Which Harappan site is located in Gujarat and is known for its water conservation systems?
questions.explanation: Dholavira in Gujarat is known for its elaborate water conservation system with large reservoirs, stepwells, and dams — unique among all Harappan sites.

questions.question: Ochre Coloured Pottery (OCP) culture preceded which period in India?
questions.explanation: OCP culture (2000-1500 BCE) in the Ganga plains preceded the Vedic period and marks the transition from the Harappan civilization to early Vedic settlements.

questions.question: Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA) was co-founded by:
questions.explanation: HSRA was reorganized by Bhagat Singh, Chandrashekhar Azad, Sukhdev, and others in 1928. They believed in socialist revolution through direct action against British imperialism.

questions.question: The first cotton textile mill in India was established at:
questions.explanation: India's first modern cotton textile mill was established in Bombay in 1854 by Cowasjee Nanabhoy Davar. This marked the beginning of modern industrialization in India.

questions.question: The word 'India' is derived from the river:
questions.explanation: The name 'India' derives from the river Indus (Sanskrit: Sindhu). Ancient Persians and Greeks called the land east of the Indus as 'Hind' or 'India'.

questions.question: The term 'Second Urbanization' in Indian history refers to:
options: Rise of cities along Ganga around 600 BCE
questions.explanation: Second Urbanization (c. 600-300 BCE) refers to the rise of large cities (Pataliputra, Vaishali, Kashi, Ujjain) in the Gangetic plains, distinct from the first urbanization of the Indus Valley.

questions.question: Firuz Shah Tughlaq is known for:
options: Welfare works — canals (including the 200km Rajasthan canal), hospitals (shifakhana), madrasas, rest houses, orchards; banned torture
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah Tughlaq (1351–1388) built extensive infrastructure: 150 wells, 100 caravanserais, 30 reservoirs/canals, 100 hospitals, 150 bridges. Banned many cruel punishments. But also discriminated against Hindus.

questions.question: The Indus Valley script has approximately how many signs?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley script has over 400 distinct signs, suggesting it is a logosyllabic system (like Egyptian hieroglyphics). It remains one of the world's great undeciphered writing systems.

questions.question: Akbar's policy of 'Sulh-i-Kul' (Universal Peace) meant:
options: Religious tolerance towards all faiths — abolishing jizya, allowing all religions to practice freely
questions.explanation: Sulh-i-Kul was Akbar's (1556–1605) policy of universal harmony — abolishing jizya (non-Muslim tax), allowing free practice of all religions, marrying Rajput princess Jodha Bai, including Hindus in court, and starting Din-i-Ilahi (1582).

questions.question: Aurangzeb's policy towards Hindus differed from Akbar's because:
options: He was more generous with Hindus
He reimposed jizya (1679), destroyed some Hindu temples, banned music, and applied strict Sharia — more orthodox Islamic policies
He gave more Hindus positions in administration
He merged Hindu and Muslim festivals
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb (1658–1707) reversed Akbar's tolerant policies: reimposed jizya (1679), destroyed some temples (Kashi Vishwanath, Mathura's Keshav Deo), banned music and dance, executed Guru Tegh Bahadur (1675). This led to Rajput and Sikh revolts.

questions.question: The Swadeshi Movement (1905) involved:
options: Boycott of British goods and promotion of Indian-made goods — also included national education and Swadesh sanghas
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi Movement arose after the Partition of Bengal (1905). It involved: burning foreign cloth, boycotting British goods, promoting Indian industries (Swadeshi), national schools, and Swadesh Sanghas. Leaders: Tilak, Bipin Chandra Pal, Lala Lajpat Rai (Lal-Bal-Pal), Aurobindo.

questions.question: The Industrial Revolution first began in:
questions.explanation: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain (England) in the 18th century (1760s-1840s) with mechanization of textile industry, steam engine, iron production, and railways.

questions.question: The Industrial Revolution began in:
questions.explanation: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain (c. 1760–1840), driven by coal, iron, steam power, and textile innovations (Spinning Jenny, power loom, steam engine by James Watt). Britain's colonial empire provided raw materials and markets.

questions.question: Which of the following sites of the Indus Valley Civilization is located in the Indian state of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Lothal is a major IVC site in Gujarat, known for its dockyard and bead-making industry.

questions.question: The Great Bath of the Indus Valley Civilization was discovered at which site?
questions.explanation: The Great Bath, a large brick-lined public bathing structure, is located at Mohenjo-daro.

questions.question: Which metal was NOT known to the people of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization was a Bronze Age culture; iron was not known to them.

questions.question: Which animal is NOT depicted on the seals of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The horse was not native to the IVC and is absent from its seals and artifacts.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization belongs to which historical period?
questions.explanation: The IVC is a Bronze Age civilization, evidenced by its use of bronze tools and weapons.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered the most distinctive feature of the Indus Valley Civilization's town planning?
questions.explanation: The IVC cities, like Mohenjo-daro, followed a systematic grid pattern with straight streets cutting at right angles.

questions.question: The famous 'Dancing Girl' bronze statue was found at which Indus Valley site?
questions.explanation: The bronze 'Dancing Girl' figurine was discovered at Mohenjo-daro.

questions.question: Which crop was NOT cultivated by the people of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: While wheat, barley, and cotton were grown, rice cultivation is not conclusively proven for the mature IVC.

questions.question: What was the primary script used by the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The IVC used a pictographic script, known as the Indus script, which remains largely undeciphered.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization declined around which approximate date?
questions.explanation: The mature phase of the IVC ended around 1900 BCE, followed by a gradual decline.

questions.question: Which is the largest known site of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: Rakhigarhi in Haryana is the largest Indus site, covering over 350 hectares.

questions.question: The Great Bath of the Indus Valley Civilization is located at which site?
questions.explanation: The Great Bath, a public bathing structure, is found at Mohenjo-daro.

questions.question: Which metal was NOT known to the Indus Valley people?
questions.explanation: Iron was not known; they used copper, bronze, gold, and silver.

questions.question: How many distinct signs are estimated in the Indus Valley script?
questions.explanation: The Indus script has about 400 distinct signs, though it remains undeciphered.

questions.question: Which Indus Valley site is known for a dockyard, suggesting maritime trade?
questions.explanation: Lothal in Gujarat has a dockyard indicating trade with Mesopotamia.

questions.question: The famous 'Dancing Girl' bronze statue was found at which site?
questions.explanation: The bronze figurine of a dancing girl was discovered at Mohenjo-daro.

questions.question: Which animal is most commonly depicted on Indus Valley seals?
questions.explanation: The 'unicorn' (a mythical one-horned bull) appears most frequently on seals.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known by which name?
questions.explanation: It is called the Harappan Civilization after the first discovered site, Harappa.

questions.question: What was the primary source of livelihood for the Indus Valley people?
questions.explanation: They practiced agriculture, growing wheat, barley, peas, and cotton.

questions.question: Which factor is widely accepted by modern scholars as the main cause of the decline of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: Recent research points to climate change causing prolonged drought as a major cause.

questions.question: Which animal is NOT commonly depicted on Indus Valley seals?
questions.explanation: The horse was not native to the Indus region and is absent from their seals and artifacts.

questions.question: What was the primary building material used in Indus Valley cities?
questions.explanation: Indus cities used standardized burnt bricks for construction, especially in public buildings.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization is considered part of which broader cultural period?
questions.explanation: It is a Chalcolithic (copper-stone) culture, using both stone and copper tools.

questions.question: Which site in Gujarat is known for having a dockyard, suggesting maritime trade?
questions.explanation: Lothal in Gujarat had a brick dockyard, indicating active sea trade with Mesopotamia.

questions.question: What was the likely script of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The Indus script was pictographic, but it remains undeciphered.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major crop grown by the Indus Valley people?
questions.explanation: Wheat and barley were staple crops; cotton was also grown but wheat was primary.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization flourished during which period?
questions.explanation: The mature phase of the Indus Valley Civilization is dated between 2500 and 1750 BCE.

questions.question: Which of the following was a characteristic feature of Indus Valley town planning?
questions.explanation: Cities like Mohenjo-daro had a grid-like layout with straight streets intersecting at right angles.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest known Indus Valley Civilization site?

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization belongs to which archaeological age?

questions.question: Which of the following was a major port city of the Indus Valley Civilization?

questions.question: The Indus Valley script is best described as:
questions.explanation: The script is pictographic, with over 400 symbols, and remains undeciphered.

questions.question: The famous bronze statue of the 'Dancing Girl' was found at which site?
questions.explanation: The 'Dancing Girl' bronze figurine was discovered at Mohenjo-daro, showcasing advanced metallurgy.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most prominent feature of the Indus Valley Civilization?
options: A. Planned cities with grid iron layout
questions.explanation: Indus Valley cities like Harappa and Mohenjo-Daro were well-planned with grid iron street patterns, advanced drainage, and standardized bricks.

questions.question: What was the primary material used for making seals in the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: Indus Valley seals were typically made of steatite (soapstone) and often engraved with animal motifs and script.

questions.question: The Indus Valley script is considered to be which type of writing system?
questions.explanation: The Indus script remains undeciphered but is generally classified as pictographic, containing symbols that represent objects or ideas.

questions.question: Which Indus Valley site is known for its elaborate water management system and reservoirs?
questions.explanation: Dholavira in Gujarat had a sophisticated water harvesting system with several reservoirs and stepwells.

questions.question: What was the likely cause of the decline of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The decline is attributed to multiple factors including repeated floods, shifts in river courses, and climate change leading to aridity.

questions.question: Which type of grain was extensively cultivated by the Indus Valley people?
questions.explanation: Wheat and barley were the staple crops; evidence of rice has also been found at some sites like Lothal.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major export item of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: Archaeological evidence suggests that Indus Valley people traded cotton textiles with Mesopotamia and other regions.

questions.question: Which of the following metals was unknown to the Indus Valley people?
questions.explanation: Iron smelting was not known to the Indus Valley Civilization; they used copper, bronze, gold, and silver.

questions.question: The Indus script is mainly found inscribed on which material?
questions.explanation: Most Indus inscriptions are on steatite seals used for trade and administrative purposes.

questions.question: Which Indus Valley site is famous for its dockyard?
questions.explanation: Lothal had a brick dockyard, indicating maritime trade and a thriving bead-making industry.

questions.question: The famous 'Pashupati seal' from Mohenjo-daro depicts a figure identified with which deity?
questions.explanation: The seal shows a three-faced figure surrounded by animals, considered a proto-Shiva or Pashupati.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization was contemporary to which of the following civilizations?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley had trade links with the Mesopotamian civilization (Magan, Dilmun).

questions.question: Which of the following animals was not domesticated by the Indus Valley people?
questions.explanation: Horse bones are absent from early Indus sites; horses were introduced later by the Aryans.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered the largest site of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: Rakhigarhi, located in present-day Haryana, is the largest known Indus Valley site, covering over 350 hectares.

questions.question: Which of the following Indus Valley sites is known for having a large dockyard?
questions.explanation: Lothal in Gujarat had a dockyard that suggests maritime trade; it is a prominent Indus Valley site.

questions.question: The Great Bath, a large public bathing structure, was found at which Indus Valley site?
questions.explanation: The Great Bath is a notable structure at Mohenjo-daro, used for ritual bathing.

questions.question: Which metal was predominantly used by the Indus Valley people for making tools and weapons?
questions.explanation: Indus Valley civilization used bronze extensively, though they also used copper; iron was unknown to them.

questions.question: The famous 'Dancing Girl' bronze statue was discovered at which Indus Valley site?
questions.explanation: The 'Dancing Girl' is a bronze figurine found at Mohenjo-daro, indicating advanced metalwork.

questions.question: Which of the following crops was first cultivated in the Indus Valley region?
questions.explanation: Cotton (Gossypium) was cultivated early in the Indus Valley, evidenced by cloth remains at Mohenjo-daro.

questions.question: The Indus Valley script is considered to be:
questions.explanation: The script is pictographic and remains undeciphered; it is not related to Brahmi or hieroglyphs.

questions.question: Which Indus Valley site is located in present-day Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Kalibangan is in Rajasthan, north of the Ghaggar river, known for fire altars and ploughed field.

questions.question: The 'Pashupati' seal from Mohenjo-daro depicts a figure that is often associated with:
questions.explanation: The seal shows a figure surrounded by animals, interpreted as a proto-Shiva (Pashupati) or a deity.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest known Indus Valley site?
questions.explanation: Rakhigarhi in Haryana is the largest Indus Valley site, covering over 350 hectares.

questions.question: The drainage system of the Indus Valley cities is best described as:
questions.explanation: Indus cities had well-planned covered drains with brick-lined channels, indicating advanced sanitation.

questions.question: Which of the following Harappan sites is located in the state of Gujarat, India?
questions.explanation: Lothal is the only Harappan site in Gujarat with a dockyard, indicating maritime trade.

questions.question: The Pashupati seal from Mohenjo-daro is generally interpreted as a depiction of which deity?
questions.explanation: The seal shows a figure surrounded by animals, often identified as Proto-Shiva or 'Lord of Beasts'.

questions.question: The Indus Valley people had an extensive trade network with which ancient civilization?
questions.explanation: Indus seals and artifacts found in Mesopotamia confirm active trade relations.

questions.question: What was the primary building material used for the drainage system in Indus cities?
questions.explanation: The Indus cities had well-planned brick-lined drains, often covered with stone or brick slabs.

questions.question: The absence of which of the following suggests that the Indus Valley society was not militaristic?
questions.explanation: The lack of large fortifications and extensive weaponry indicates a relatively peaceful society.

questions.question: Which Indus site provides evidence of a dockyard, indicating maritime trade?
questions.explanation: Lothal in Gujarat has a dockyard, showing the Indus people engaged in maritime trade.

questions.question: What material was predominantly used to make the Indus Valley seals?
questions.explanation: Most Indus seals were made from steatite, a soft stone that was carved and fired.

questions.question: The presence of the Great Granary at Mohenjo-daro indicates that the Indus people had:
questions.explanation: The Great Granary suggests surplus grain storage, pointing to a strong agricultural base.

questions.question: The Indus Valley script has not been deciphered, but some scholars hypothesize it belongs to which language family?
questions.explanation: Many scholars link the Indus script to the Dravidian language family based on substrate evidence.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization did not know iron smelting; it came later during the Vedic period.

questions.question: The Great Bath of Mohenjodaro was primarily used for what purpose?
questions.explanation: The Great Bath is believed to have been used for ritualistic purification, a key feature of Harappan society.

questions.question: Which animal is NOT depicted on Harappan seals?
questions.explanation: Common seal animals include unicorn, tiger, elephant, and rhinoceros; zebra is absent.

questions.question: The Indus script is best described as:

questions.question: Which of the following Indus Valley sites is located in present-day Gujarat?

questions.question: With which contemporary civilization did the Indus Valley people engage in trade?
questions.explanation: Evidence of trade with Mesopotamia (Sumer) has been found through seals, beads, and other artifacts.

questions.question: What was the primary staple crop of the Indus Valley civilization?
questions.explanation: Wheat and barley were the staple crops; rice was also known but less common.

questions.question: The most common material used for Harappan seals was:
questions.explanation: Most Harappan seals were made of steatite (soft stone), often baked for hardness.

questions.question: Which feature is NOT characteristic of Harappan town planning?
questions.explanation: Harappan cities had rectangular houses; circular houses were not a typical feature.

questions.question: The famous bronze 'Dancing Girl' statue was found at which site?
questions.explanation: The bronze Dancing Girl was discovered at Mohenjodaro, dating to circa 2500 BCE.

questions.question: Which Indus Valley site is known for its elaborate water management system, including a large reservoir and stepwell?
questions.explanation: Dholavira in Gujarat had a sophisticated water conservation system with reservoirs and a stepwell, unique among Indus sites.

questions.question: Which metal was the first to be used extensively by the people of the Indus Valley Civilization?

questions.question: Which of the following animals is NOT found on the Pashupati seal of the Indus Valley Civilization?

questions.question: What was the most common building material used for houses in the Indus Valley cities?

questions.explanation: The script uses many pictographic signs, though some are abstract, leading to its classification as primarily pictographic.
